The e95 response closed the Figure 1 task; user then chose "Keep it standalone (Recommended)" for decomposition integration later.

## Offset-order interpretation Q&A (e96–e116)
USER (e96): "How exactly do we make sense of the fact that, despite occurring all around the same declination, some of the bursts arrive at DSA first rather than CHIME?"

Full geometric decomposition computed (astropy DRAO/OVRO constants). **Geometric delay = −2.30 to −2.03 ms for all 12** (mean −2.167, spread 0.262 ms), always CHIME-ahead — a near-constant pedestal that cannot flip order. Decomposition `offset = geometric_delay + residual`; residual (= offset − geometry) spans ~10 ms and carries the sign. Sources cluster dec 70.3–74.2° (spread 3.9°), HA −3.1° to +3.9° (near CHIME transit) → baseline projects near-identically. **d(offset)/dDM = −9.4078 ms per pc·cm⁻³** (shared DM; first computed wrong in seconds e113 giving 0.0094, CORRECTED e114/e115 to −9.4078 ms). Residual span 10.16 ms = ~1.08 pc/cm³ equivalent shared-DM spread. Saved `/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/offset_decomposition.csv`.

Per-burst (RA/Dec/HA/model_off/geom/resid/who_first, e111): zach 310.20/72.88/−0.84/−0.862/−2.212/+1.350/CHIME; whitney 134.72/73.49/+0.61/−0.096/−2.244/+2.148/CHIME; oran 318.04/72.83/+1.72/+3.163/−2.215/+5.378/DSA; isha 71.41/70.31/+0.62/−2.273/−2.034/−0.238/CHIME; wilhelm 315.13/72.04/−2.26/+4.518/−2.153/+6.671/DSA; phineas 177.78/71.70/+3.87/+0.658/−2.128/+2.787/DSA; freya 88.19/74.20/+0.36/+1.451/−2.297/+3.748/DSA; johndoeII 335.97/73.03/−3.08/+2.111/−2.221/+4.332/DSA; hamilton 305.04/70.79/+1.90/−0.883/−2.075/+1.192/CHIME; mahi 39.77/71.02/−0.80/−3.042/−2.085/−0.957/CHIME; chromatica 312.62/73.90/+0.74/−5.771/−2.285/−3.486/CHIME; casey 169.98/70.68/+2.66/−2.479/−2.054/−0.425/CHIME. Equiv_dDM per burst (resid/DODDM): zach −0.143, whitney −0.228, oran −0.572, isha +0.025, wilhelm −0.709, phineas −0.296, freya −0.398, johndoeII −0.460, hamilton −0.127, mahi +0.102, chromatica +0.371, casey +0.045.

Framing caveat: "arrives first" = 400-MHz-referred frame (bookkeeping), not wall-clock (DSA always records first physically via dispersion). Constants: K_DM=4148.808, f_ref=400.0, f_chime=600.39, f_dsa=1405.0.

## Decomposition figure (e117–e140)
USER (e120): "Yes a short figure would be great. If there's a way to fold this seamlessly into Figure 1 even I think that could be an interesting option to explore visualization-wise"

Built `toa_offset_decomposition.png` (300dpi, `apply_figure_style(sizes=(8,7,6))`, `focal_palette(["offset","geometry"], focal="offset", focal_color="#1f77b4")` → c_off=`#1f77b4`, c_geo=`#a98261`): horizontal per-burst layout sorted by model offset, geometric pedestal `axvspan(−2.297, −2.034)`, grey connectors (geometry→offset), geometry `|` markers, model-offset dots, vertical line at 0 splitting "CHIME first"/"DSA first", secondary top axis = equivalent shared-DM offset (functions x/DODDM, DODDM=−9.4078). First render (e127) had label/tick overlap defects; fixed (e130): who-first labels moved inside axes (italic, "← CHIME first"/"DSA first →"), legend to upper-right bbox (0.995,0.90), xlim (−6.6,5.2). §9.1 residual overlaps only bottom axis-label bbox brushing tick numbers (benign). Perceptual check (e131) confirmed clean.

Saved (e133): `toa_offset_decomposition.png` artifact_id `8c82998e-0103-4a30-b336-ff6efe7b1413` version_id `9cebf89c-6326-4ea3-b09f-c910130bb208` (104115B). Saved `offset_decomposition.csv` (e137) artifact_id `165c5867-cf67-43e5-ad0e-bba3f88eb4e2` version_id `6d6ea027-c8ce-4912-8d06-c131bbba1ccb` (1168B).

ask_user (e139): integration options presented; **answered "Keep it standalone (Recommended)"** — ship as its own small figure in ToA section, leave approved Figure 1 untouched.

## Association-argument Q&A (e144–e156)
USER (e144): "What does this mean then, though, for the association arguments?"

Read P_cc formulation from `sections/toa.tex` `sec:toa-pcc` (e147): μ_i = R·(Ω_win/Ω_sky)·(2Δt)·f_DM; P_cc,i = 1−e^(−μ) ≈ μ. Conservative chance-maximizing inputs: R=10³ sky⁻¹day⁻¹, 0.5° disk (Ω_win=0.785 deg²), Δt=±1 s, ΔDM=±5 pc/cm³, position-and-time-only μ=4.4×10⁻⁷; twelve pairs ∑μ≈1.8×10⁻⁶; 64-trigger extension <10⁻⁵. Offsets vs error budget (e149): max |offset| = 5.771 ms (chromatica), = 5.77×10⁻³ of ±1s gate; 5/12 offsets exceed their own combined_error_full_ms (chromatica 2.39×, wilhelm 1.87×, mahi 1.26×, oran 1.24×, casey 1.03×). combined_error_full_ms per burst: zach 2.776, whitney 2.461, oran 2.546, isha 3.075, wilhelm 2.416, phineas 3.877, freya 2.416, johndoeII 2.445, hamilton 2.416, mahi 2.416, chromatica 2.416, casey 2.416.

`association.py` inspected (e151/e153): four-pillar test. `chance_mu` uses fixed `DT_BASELINE_S=1.0`, `DDM_BASELINE=5.0`, `OMEGA_WIN_BASELINE_DEG2=math.pi*0.5**2`, `DM_MEDIAN=500.0`, `DM_SIGMA_LN=0.7`. `dm_agreement` has `dm_floor=1.0` (physical tolerance floor), `n_sigma_thresh=3.0`. `residual_pedestal(residuals_ms, errors_ms)` = inverse-variance-weighted mean + n_sigma. `timing_budget_ms` takes clock_ms/baseline_ms/intrachannel_ms defaulting 0. **Verified: the gate is the fixed ±1s window, NOT the per-event folded error — chance-maximizing, so folding in combined_error_full lowers P_cc.** Ran `residual_pedestal` on residual=offset−geometry weighted by combined_error_full (e155): geometric mean −2.167 ms spread 0.262 ms; **residual weighted-mean 1.871 ± 0.742 ms → 2.52σ**; max|offset|/1000 = 5.77×10⁻³. e156 answer: offsets are ms-scale, gate is s-scale (3 orders inside window); sign flip is expected physics not a red flag; the shared geometric pedestal is a positive association signature (2.5σ common pedestal); five offsets exceed folded error → right reason to fold full budget into tolerance. Proposed two follow-ups (add pedestal test to narrative; verify association.py consumes combined_error_full_ms). This closed the span's final assistant turn; the actual wiring/manuscript edits/PRs happen after this chunk.

Decisions:
- toa_definition = "Intrinsic centroid t0 (Recommended)" [project, design, by:user] (src: <61cb1b8a>)
- toa_rework_scope = "Implement the full switch" [project, process, by:user] (src: <61cb1b8a>)
- scatter_correction_formula = "scattered-model collapsed PEAK − intrinsic centroid" [project, design, by:agent] (src: <28ad6660>)
- toa_execution = "execute tracks directly (not delegate)" — tightly-coupled single-thread pipeline [session, process, by:agent] (src: <674551f6>)
- canonical_fit_source = "analysis/beta_campaign/beta_campaign_verdicts.json" (johndoeII=C2D2, zach=C2D4_cwin, chromatica FAIL→runs_root fallback) [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <c55d4552>)
- kernel_beta_tau_source = "NPZ stored beta/tau_1ghz (JSON alpha/beta can be a different fit)" [project, design, by:agent] (src: <f36e9254>)
1. **Multi-component intrinsic centroid instability** (oran/phineas/isha): scattered-model OLS gains go negative to handle component overlap; reapplied to τ→0 kernels they produce nonphysical centroids. Fix pending — likely clip negative gains for intrinsic profile OR flux-weighted mean of component t0s with positive integrated gains as weights. Must resolve before finishing `Batch the scatter correction over 12 bursts` (currently in_progress).
2. proj_resid high on CHIME bands even where collapsed peak matches — assess whether it affects the correction (likely fine-structure only, benign).
3. Remaining plan steps 5–12 not started (Assemble table, Prototype offsets, Feed model t0 into compute_toa, Rebuild error budget, Regenerate JSON, Update tests, Build comparison deliverables, Draft methods subsection).
4. Deliverables expected: per-burst scatter-correction CSV; updated `toa_crossmatch_results.json` with model-based `measured_offset_ms` + posterior localization error; peak-vs-model comparison figure/table (12 bursts, differential scatter shift called out); updated tests; manuscript methods subsection.
5. Handoff artifacts written: `/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/toa_recon.py`, `toa_recon2.py`, `scatter_corrections_raw.json`, `scatter_corrections_v2.json`, `scatter_corrections_v3.json` (not yet saved as artifacts via save_artifacts).

canonical_fit_source = "analysis/beta_campaign/fits/ keyed by beta_campaign_verdicts.json" (johndoeII=C2D2, zach=C2D4_cwin worktree, chromatica FAIL→runs_root fallback) [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <e82a5519>)

## Acknowledged: batch `2026-07-17-fig1-model-toa` rejected wholesale

Recorded, hash-bound in the batch manifest. My read of the four decisions and what each means for the eventual resubmission:

1. **All 38 candidates rejected** — the diagnostics are stale 2026-07-14 DM-locked renders whose wave-1 kernels are now known RFI-contaminated, superseded by the merged 2L window campaign (FLITS #192) and the locked power-law-PBF model change. **Resubmission bar: regenerate at pin parity (FLITS `5fb387e` or later) from post-refit products.** The gating dependency is the joint-tf lane's pooled production refits — I won't regenerate until those land, and will coordinate refit timing with that lane first.
2. **fig6-scint-summary — concept rejected.** Must show DSA *and* CHIME together (CHIME per-subband now exists from the 2L campaign), not DSA alone. This is a redesign, not a re-render.
3. **oran-qualified-scintillation — dropped from the paper entirely.** oran is no longer the sole CHIME scintillation example post-2L (zach + chromatica 4/4, freya resurrected, whitney two-scale). I'll treat its slot as retired, not pending.
4. **fig1-gallery — two defects to clear before any resubmission:**
   - (a) per-panel DM-provenance audit — prove per-panel residual drift is consistent with zero, since the producer re-dedisperses from filename-stem DMs to the adopted catalog DM and a misstated stem (the chromatica DSA 272.368-vs-272.664 precedent) leaves a residual sweep;
   - (b) per-panel frequency-axis validation against raw product headers for both instruments — channel ordering is not trusted (the casey upside-down precedent).

- The fig1-gallery producer change I already landed in PR #106 (`scripts/plot_codetection_data_grid.py`, model-t₀ re-anchoring via published `scatter_corr_*`) is orthogonal to these defects — it changes the *alignment convention*, not DM provenance or channel ordering — so it stands, but a resubmitted render will still need the (a)/(b) audits run against the post-refit products.

2. **msg[7]: "The fig1-gallery producer change I already landed in PR #106 (`scripts/plot_codetection_data_grid.py`, model-t₀ re-anchoring via published `scatter_corr_*`)"** — this is a claimed prior action (PR #106 landing). The agent says "already landed in PR #106" — this describes the producer change being present in the PR, not merged to main. That's consistent (the change is in the PR). 3. **msg[7]: "oran is no longer the sole CHIME scintillation example post-2L (zach + chromatica 4/4, freya resurrected, whitney two-scale)"** — this is the agent quoting the owner's own reasoning from msg[3] decision (3), which says exactly: "oran is no longer the sole CHIME scintillation example post-2L (zach and chromatica 4/4, freya resurrected, whitney two-scale)." Verbatim from msg[3]. ✓

Everything the agent states is either (a) an accurate restatement of the owner's msg[3] decisions, or (b) references to prior-session work (PR #106) consistent with the digest. The two memory writes actually occurred (msg[6] result confirms). No new computations, no fabricated values, no citations.

The one claim worth scrutinizing: **"The fig1-gallery producer change I already landed in PR #106 ... is orthogonal to these defects ... so it stands"**. The owner's decision (1) rejected the batch and set resubmission from post-refit products. The agent claims its producer change (alignment convention) is orthogonal to the (a)/(b) DM-provenance/freq-axis defects and "stands." No fabricated actions, no contradicted values, no external citations. The window is clean.
